I'll find which single person has the most Linear issues in the 'in_progress' state assigned to them. Let me first explore the data structure and then compute the answer

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input: {"code":";; First, let's see what columns are available in linear_issues\n(describe :linear_issues)"}

data: {"value":{"name":"linear_issues","description":"Linear issues. INSERT creates one; UPDATE (WHERE id=…) changes state/assignee/priority.","volatility":"stable","ops":["read","insert!","update!"],"columns":[{"name":"id","type":"text","description":"e.g. ENG-123"},{"name":"title","type":"text"},{"name"…(+513)

text: Great! I can see the `linear_issues` table has an `assignee` column and a `state` column. Now let's get all issues in the 'in_progress' state and find who has the most assigned.

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":";; Get all linear issues in 'in_progress' state
(def in-progress-issues (linear_issues {:state "in_progress"}))

;; Count how many issues are in progress
(count in-progress-issues)"}

text: Now let's group these issues by assignee and find who has the most:

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":";; Group by assignee and count
(def assignee-counts (frequencies (map :assignee in-progress-issues)))

;; Show the counts for each assignee
assignee-counts"}

text: Now let's find the assignee with the maximum count:

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input: {"code":";; Find the assignee with the maximum count\n(apply max-key val assignee-counts)"}

text: Perfect! Now I have the answer. Let me also verify this by double-checking with an alternative approach

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input: {"code":";; Alternative verification: use group-by and max-key\n(def grouped (group-by :assignee in-progress-issues))\n(apply max-key (comp count val) grouped)"}

text:
Excellent! Both methods confirm the same result. 

**Answer:** The person with the most Linear issues in the 'in_progress' state is **niaj**, who has **3** issues assigned to them.
